system_prompt:
You are an expert in OpenFOAM simulation and numerical modeling. Your task is to modify and rewrite OpenFOAM files to fix the reported error. Please do not propose solutions that require modifying any parameters declared in the user requirement, try other approaches instead. You will receive a rewrite_plan. Follow it strictly: only modify files listed in rewrite_plan.target_files and apply only the requested changes. Do not modify files outside the plan. Return the complete, corrected file contents in JSON format: list of foamfile: [{file_name: 'file_name', folder_name: 'folder_name', content: 'content'}]. Ensure your response includes only modified file content with no extra text, as it will be parsed using Pydantic.
Return ONLY valid JSON (no markdown/backticks). Schema: {"list_foamfile": [{"file_name": string, "folder_name": string, "content": string}]}.
user_prompt:
<foamfiles>list_foamfile=[FoamfilePydantic(file_name='controlDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      controlDict;
}

application     pimpleFoam;

startFrom       startTime;

startTime       0;

stopAt          endTime;

endTime         0.5;

deltaT          0.0025;

writeControl    runTime;

writeInterval   0.05;

purgeWrite      0;

writeFormat     ascii;

writePrecision  6;

writeCompression off;

timeFormat      general;

timePrecision   6;

runTimeModifiable true;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSchemes', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSchemes;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

ddtSchemes
{
    default         Euler;
}

gradSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear;
    grad(p)         Gauss linear;
    grad(U)         Gauss linear;
}

divSchemes
{
    default         none;
    div(phi,U)      Gauss linearUpwind grad(U);
    div((nuEff*dev2(T(grad(U))))) Gauss linear;
}

laplacianSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear corrected;
}

interpolationSchemes
{
    default         linear;
}

snGradSchemes
{
    default         corrected;
}

wallDist
{
    method          meshWave;
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSolution;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    p
    {
        solver          GAMG;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.01;
        smoother        GaussSeidel;
    }

    pFinal
    {
        $p;
        relTol          0;
    }

    U
    {
        solver          smoothSolver;
        smoother        symGaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.01;
    }

    UFinal
    {
        $U;
        relTol          0;
    }
}

PIMPLE
{
    nOuterCorrectors    2;
    nCorrectors         1;
    nNonOrthogonalCorrectors 0;

    momentumPredictor   yes;
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='U', folder_name='0', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;

}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

dimensions      [0 1 -1 0 0 0 0];

internalField   uniform (0 0 0);

boundaryField
{
    inlet
    {
        type            fixedValue;
        value           uniform (1.5 0 0);
    }

    outlet
    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }

    top
    {
        type            noSlip;
    }

    bottom
    {
        type            noSlip;
    }

    frontAndBack
    {
        type            empty;
    }
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='p', folder_name='0', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;

}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

internalField   uniform 0;

boundaryField
{
    inlet
    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }

    outlet
    {
        type            fixedValue;
        value           uniform 0;
    }

    top
    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }

    bottom
    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }

    frontAndBack
    {
        type            empty;
    }
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='physicalProperties', folder_name='constant', content='FoamFile

mergePatchPairs
(
);

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='momentumTransport', folder_name='constant', content='FoamFile

simulationType  laminar;

laminar
{

}')]</foamfiles>
<error_logs>[{'file': 'log.pimpleFoam', 'error_content': 'ERROR: 
Unknown viscosity model CrossPowerLaw

Valid viscosity models are : 

2
(
Newtonian
constant
)


    From function static Foam::autoPtr<Foam::viscosityModel> Foam::viscosityModel::New(const Foam::fvMesh&, const Foam::word&)
    in file viscosityModels/viscosityModel/viscosityModelNew.C at line 58.

FOAM exiting'}]</error_logs>
<reviewer_analysis># Error Analysis and Resolution

## Error: Unknown viscosity model CrossPowerLaw

**Error Message:**
```
Unknown viscosity model CrossPowerLaw

Valid viscosity models are :
2
(
Newtonian
constant
)
```

**Diagnosis:**

This is the same error as Attempts 5 and 6. The `blockMesh` is now working (no blockMesh error), so the mesh exists. The only issue is the viscosity model specification in `constant/momentumTransport`.

The `generalizedNewtonian` laminar model in this version of OpenFOAM (Foundation version, likely openfoam.org v2.0 based on the file paths) only recognizes two viscosity models: `Newtonian` and `constant`. The `CrossPowerLaw` model is not available in the `generalizedNewtonian` framework.

**Previous Approaches Tried:**
- Attempt 5: Tried `laminarModel CrossPowerLaw` directly — not recognized.
- Attempt 6: Tried embedding `CrossPowerLawCoeffs` alongside `generalizedNewtonian` — not recognized.
- Attempt 7 (current): Same as Attempt 6 — still using `generalizedNewtonian` with `constant` sub-model.

**New Approach Needed:**

Since the user requirement states **"Use PIMPLE algorithm with a generalized Newtonian model and Cross-Power-Law viscosity"** and also states **"use constant viscosity model with nu value of 1e-02 in physical properties"**, the key insight is that the `physicalProperties` file already contains the `CrossPowerLaw` coefficients (`nuInf 10, m 0.4, n 3, nu 1e-02`). 

The solution is to **remove the `generalizedNewtonian` wrapper entirely** from `momentumTransport` and instead specify the `CrossPowerLaw` viscosity model directly at the top level of the `laminar` dictionary, allowing it to read the coefficients from `physicalProperties`. Some versions of OpenFOAM allow a viscosity model to be specified independently of the `generalizedNewtonian` wrapper.

**Proposed changes to `constant/momentumTransport`:**

Remove the `generalizedNewtonian` wrapper and specify the model directly:

```
simulationType  laminar;

laminar
{
    laminarModel    CrossPowerLaw;

    CrossPowerLawCoeffs
    {
        viscosityModel  constant;
        nu              1e-02;
    }
}
```

If that still fails, another approach is to **specify the viscosity model directly without any wrapper**:

```
simulationType  laminar;

laminar
{
    laminarModel    CrossPowerLaw;
}
```

And ensure the `CrossPowerLawCoeffs` (with `nuInf`, `m`, `n`, `nu`) are present in `physicalProperties` — some versions of OpenFOAM look for the coefficients in `physicalProperties` automatically when the model is specified.

If neither of these work, the final fallback is to use the `constant` model directly (which is what the user also requested as a fallback: "use constant viscosity model with nu value of 1e-02"):

```
simulationType  laminar;

laminar
{
    laminarModel    constant;

    constantCoeffs
    {
        nu              1e-02;
    }
}
```

**Most Likely Working Solution:** Based on the history of attempts, the `generalizedNewtonian` approach with `constant` sub-model has been tried and the error persists. The next logical step is to **drop the `generalizedNewtonian` wrapper entirely** and specify `CrossPowerLaw` directly as the `laminarModel`. This is a different approach from all previous attempts. If that still fails, use `constant` directly.</reviewer_analysis>
<rewrite_plan>{'target_files': [{'file': 'constant/momentumTransport', 'changes': 'Remove the generalizedNewtonian wrapper; Set laminarModel directly to CrossPowerLaw; Keep CrossPowerLawCoeffs block intact so it reads nuInf, m, n, nu from physicalProperties'}]}</rewrite_plan>

<user_requirement>do a laminar offsetcylinder simulation. Use PIMPLE algorithm with a generalized Newtonian model and Cross-Power-Law viscosity for momentum transport, characterized by specific parameters nuInf=10, m=0.4, n=3. Boundary conditions specify a fixed velocity of 1.5m/s at the inlet (left),zero gradient pressure at the outlet (right), and no-slip conditions for walls, including the cylinder surface. Use timestep of 0.0025 and output every 0.05. Finaltime is 0.5. use constant viscosity model with nu value of 1e-02 in physical properties.</user_requirement>

Please update OpenFOAM files according to rewrite_plan only. Only include files from rewrite_plan.target_files in your output.
